title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction · Type-System Trace: Selector Contract
summary: A broad selector takes the first node, missing and empty values collapse together, strings are cast into domain types, and markup drift silently corrupts output. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts selector contract increment in runtime-validated extraction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Bound source, nodes, depth, attributes, text, selector work, and parse duration and expose rejection causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Selector Contract · Theory Selector Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 4295 during a multilingual archive markup migration. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove selector cardinality, missingness, unknown runtime validation, field normalization, and drift alarms; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then inject one authenticated, private-data, output-injection, path, log, plugin, or correction fault and contain it. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
